title: Half-Staff on the Fourth

mood.label: Uneasy calm 🌥️
mood.summary: An Independence Day held a notch below neutral — grief, siege and a punishing heat dome pressing down while quiet science and diplomacy keep the day from sinking.

style.genre: dub-tinged post-rock / cinematic — sampled grand piano laying slow open aeolian chords under a swelling bed of bowed strings, a brushed RhythmAce kit marching soft and spacious through deep tape-echo, a plucked upright bass and a low sine sub holding the floor, with sparse glockenspiel glints for the quiet wins; Bb aeolian's plain natural minor sitting heavy and steady, grief held rather than dramatized on a hot, flag-lowered Fourth

sampleSources: Strudel default library (sampled piano, VCSL strings & mallets, RhythmAce)
pattern: stack( s("~ ~ rim ~").bank("RhythmAce").gain(0.28).room(0.3), s("hh*8").bank("RhythmAce").gain(perlin.range(0.05,0.16)).pan(sine.range(0.35,0.65).slow(9)).hpf(6000).delay(0.25).delaytime(0.375), note("<[bf2,df3,f3] [gf2,bf2,df3] [ef2,gf2,bf2] [f2,af2,c3]>").s("piano").attack(0.02).release(1.4).gain(0.34).lpf(sine.range(1400,2600).slow(16)).room(0.42).rsize(4), note("<[bf3,df4,f4] [gf3,bf3,df4] [ef3,gf3,bf3] [f3,af3,c4]>").s("strings").attack(0.9).sustain(0.8).release(2.6).gain(0.2).lpf(2000).room(0.55).rsize(5), note("bf1 ~ bf1 f1 ef1 ~ f1 ~").s("pizzicato").gain(0.3).lpf(900).room(0.25).off(0.5, x => x.gain(0.12)), note("bf0 ~ ~ ~ ef0 ~ f0 ~").s("sine").gain(0.34).lpf(180).attack(0.03).release(0.7), note("~ f4 ~ df4 ~ bf4 ~ ~").s("glockenspiel").gain(0.16).room(0.5).delay(0.3).delaytime(0.5).pan(0.62).slow(2) )

lines: Flags come down half a pole on a hundred-and-five degree street,
grid lights flicker like a pulse that the heat won't let complete.
Somewhere a mosque holds a farewell twenty-four hours long,
and the talks just pause for the week — not broken, just not strong.
It's an uneasy calm, forty-four and holding low,
grief on one shoulder, on the other a quiet glow.
War and siege and a heat that won't break the sky,
but the powder stops the bleeding, and a trial gives one more try.
Al Obeid under a shadow, half a million holding on,
Kyiv counts the morning after, Crimea's calm long gone.
Still Tokyo and Delhi shake hands over silicon and sea,
and a brain-cancer cell just vanished in a lab where it could be.
It's an uneasy calm, forty-four and holding low,
grief on one shoulder, on the other a quiet glow.
The Fourth burns hot and the flags stay down,
but the science keeps its footing on the far side of the frown.
